metadata.notes: Tier 3 covers firms ranked 19-24 by AUM, including target company PAX. PAX receives maximum detail with FY2025 as FY1. Some firms (Eurazeo, Antin) have not yet released FY2025 full-year results; most recent available data used. P10 rebranded as Ridgepost Capital in Feb 2026.

firms.data_quality_notes: Target company — maximum detail. FY2025 annual results released Feb 3, 2026. Reports in USD but significant BRL-denominated fee base. FY2022 base-year data sourced from earnings presentations (beginning of 2022: $24B total AUM, $18B FEAUM).

Majority-alt classification. Wendel is transitioning from a holding company to third-party asset manager via acquisitions of IK Partners (2023) and Monroe Capital (2024). FY2025 results released Feb 26, 2026. Reports in EUR. FRE figures include only the WIM (Wendel Investment Managers) platform, not principal investments. FPAuM growth of 207% is largely inorganic (acquisition-driven).

Majority-alt classification. 3i operates as a principal investor (balance sheet-driven model), not a traditional third-party fund manager. FY ends March 31. FY2025 = year to March 31, 2025. Infrastructure AUM of £6.3B is the third-party managed component; PE is principally on balance sheet (Action is dominant holding). Traditional alt-manager metrics (FRE, FEAUM, DE/share) do not apply in standard form. Data collected where applicable but many metrics null due to business model mismatch.

Majority-alt classification. Hybrid model: own balance sheet + third-party AUM. FY2025 full-year results expected March 11, 2026 (not yet available). H1 2025 and 9M 2025 data used as most recent. FY2024 full-year data available. Reports in EUR.

Pure-play alt. Rebranded from P10 to Ridgepost Capital on Feb 11, 2026, new ticker RPC. FY2025 results released Feb 12, 2026. Reports in USD. Acquired Stellus Capital Management (direct lending) in early 2026.

Pure-play infrastructure alt manager. FY2025 full-year results not yet released (expected March 2026). H1 2025 and FY2024 data used. Reports in EUR. ~98% of revenue from long-term contracted management fees. FY ends December 31.

data_points.extraction_notes: DE of $200.9M, $1.27 per share for FY2025. Up 6% from FY2024.
Implied from FY2025 DE/share of $1.27 being 6% higher than FY2024. Approximate: $1.27/1.06 = ~$1.20.

data_points.source_description: MacroTrends / Stock data aggregators — PAX EPS TTM
data_points.extraction_notes: GAAP EPS (TTM) of $0.54 as reported. IFRS net income of $85.6M for FY2025. Q4 2025 EPS was $0.50 (likely includes one-time items).

data_points.missing_reason: Requires FY2022 DE/share as base. PAX began with ~$24B total AUM at start of 2022. Precise FY2022 DE/share not available from search results. Cannot compute CAGR without base year.

data_points.extraction_notes: FEAUM of $40.8B as of December 31, 2025. Up 24% YoY from $33.0B at YE2024.
FEAUM of $33.0B as of December 31, 2024. Up 38% YoY. Confirmed in FY2025 release as base.

data_points.source_description: Patria earnings presentations — historical reference to beginning of 2022
data_points.extraction_notes: Beginning of 2022 FEAUM was $18B. End-of-year FY2022 figure may differ. Used as approximate baseline for CAGR calculation.
Total AUM of $52.6B as of December 31, 2025. Up 26% from FY2024.
Total AUM at YE2024. Implied from 26% growth to reach $52.6B: $52.6B / 1.26 = ~$41.7B.

data_points.source_description: Patria earnings presentations — historical reference to beginning of 2022
data_points.extraction_notes: Beginning of 2022 total AUM was $24B. End-of-year figure may differ.
FEAUM grew 24% YoY from $33.0B to $40.8B. Driven by organic capital inflows of $5.6B and acquisitions.
FEAUM grew 38% YoY in FY2024 to $33B.
Approximate 3Y CAGR: ($40.8/$18)^(1/3) - 1 = ~31%. Base-year is beginning-of-2022 estimate. Actual end-of-FY2022 FEAUM may differ slightly.
Record organic fundraising of $7.7B in 2025. Beginning AUM ~$41.7B. Ratio: $7.7B/$41.7B = ~18.5%. Rounded to 0.18.

data_points.missing_reason: Detailed asset class AUM breakdown (PE, infrastructure, credit, real estate, solutions, public equities) not available from public search results. PAX reports across multiple segments but exact AUM per segment not found. Known to have broad diversification across infrastructure, credit, RE, PE, solutions (GPMS), and public equities.

data_points.metric_name: Permanent Capital as Percentage of AUM
data_points.source_description: BeyondSPX analysis of Patria; Investor presentations
data_points.extraction_notes: Permanent capital vehicles represent ~22% of total FEAUM pro-forma following recent acquisitions. PAX restructured from <10% NAV-based FEAUM at IPO to >60% currently (NAV-based, not necessarily permanent capital). Permanent capital figure is approximate.

data_points.missing_reason: Exact credit AUM as percentage not disclosed in public search results. PAX is expanding into credit via Solis Investimentos acquisition (Jan 2026) and existing credit platform. Credit is a growing but not yet dominant segment.

data_points.extraction_notes: Full-year FRE margin of 58.9% (some sources report 58.5%). Q4 2025 margin was 63.6%. FRE of $202.5M on fee revenue base. Margin expanded ~500bps YoY.
FRE margin of 57% for FY2024. FRE of $170.1M.
FRE grew 19% YoY from $170.1M (FY2024) to $202.5M (FY2025).
FRE grew 15% YoY from $147.7M (FY2023) to $170.1M (FY2024).

data_points.missing_reason: Effective blended management fee rate in bps not explicitly disclosed in public search results. Can be approximated once fee revenue and average FEAUM are available from 20-F filing.

data_points.metric_name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
data_points.missing_reason: Breakdown of performance fees vs. management fees not available from public search results. DE of $200.9M vs FRE of $202.5M suggests minimal realized performance revenue net of compensation in FY2025.
Precise FY2025 headcount not confirmed. Approximately 842 employees as of Sept 2024. Awaiting 20-F for year-end 2025 figure.

Compensation breakdown not available from public search results. Total operating expenses for FY2025 were $141.6M (up 8% YoY) but comp vs. G&A split not disclosed in earnings release. Awaiting 20-F filing.

data_points.metric_name: General and Administrative Expense to FEAUM
data_points.missing_reason: G&A expense breakdown not available from public search results. Awaiting 20-F filing for comp vs. G&A split.

data_points.metric_name: Operating Expense Growth Minus Fee Revenue Growth
data_points.source_description: Patria Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release; Patria revenue data
data_points.extraction_notes: OpEx grew 8% YoY ($141.6M). Revenue grew ~14% (from $374.2M FY2024, implied ~19% FRE growth on fee base). Approximate spread: 8% - 19% = -11%, indicating positive operating leverage. FRE margin expansion of ~500bps corroborates.

data_points.missing_reason: PAX does not separately disclose constant-currency revenue growth. Significant BRL/USD exposure: average 2025 rate ~5.59 BRL/USD. BRL depreciated materially vs USD in 2025, suggesting reported USD growth understates local-currency growth. FX_MATERIAL flag set.

data_points.extraction_notes: Market cap ~$2.09B (as of early March 2026, share price ~$13.13). LTM FRE of $202.5M. P/FRE = $2,090M / $202.5M = ~10.3x.
Market cap ~$2.09B. LTM DE of $200.9M. P/DE = $2,090M / $200.9M = ~10.4x.

data_points.missing_reason: Enterprise value requires net debt figure from balance sheet. Market cap of $2.09B is available, but total debt minus cash not confirmed from search results. Awaiting 20-F filing for balance sheet data.

data_points.source_description: Exchange rate data providers; Federal Reserve H.10
data_points.extraction_notes: Average 2025 BRL/USD rate: ~0.179 (i.e., 1 BRL = ~0.179 USD, or ~5.59 BRL per 1 USD). BRL depreciated materially in 2025. FX_MATERIAL: true for PAX given significant BRL-denominated fee base. Impact on USD-reported metrics exceeds 500bps threshold.
data_points.missing_reason: Contextual FX data — not a standard metric. Documented here per instructions for PAX FX analysis.
Wendel does not report Distributable Earnings in the alt-manager framework. Operates as a holding company transitioning to asset management. Reports NAV and net income, not DE/share.

data_points.extraction_notes: FPAuM (Fee-Paying AUM) of €31.0B as of December 31, 2025, FX-adjusted. Up 207% YTD (largely inorganic — IK Partners + Monroe Capital acquisitions).
Total AUM of €41.2B (of which €10.6B dry powder). Upon completion of Committed Advisors acquisition, platform will reach €47B AUM.
FPAuM up 207% YTD. Largely inorganic — driven by IK Partners and Monroe Capital acquisitions consolidated during the year. Organic FPAuM growth was 13%.
Organic FPAuM growth of 13% reported separately from total 207% growth. IK Partners and Monroe GPs raised >€11B through closed-end funds since joining Wendel.

data_points.source_description: Wendel FY2025 Results Release; Analyst presentation Feb 26, 2026
data_points.extraction_notes: FRE margin of 39% (proforma 12 months). Reported FRE of €139.5M (reported) and €159M (proforma 12 months). Management fees of €349M (reported) / €404M (proforma). Proforma margin: €159M/€404M ≈ 39%.
Reported FRE up 146% YoY to €139.5M. Largely driven by acquisitions. Proforma FRE of €159M (full 12-month basis).

data_points.missing_reason: Wendel's market cap of €3.72B reflects the entire group (holding company + asset management platform). FRE of €139.5M is only the WIM platform. P/FRE on WIM alone would be misleading without adjusting for principal investment NAV. Not computed to avoid apples-to-oranges comparison.

3i Group does not report DE/share in the alt-manager framework. Operates as a principal investor (balance sheet model). Reports total return, NAV per share, and dividends. Total return was £5,049M (25% on opening shareholders' funds) for FY2025 (year to March 31, 2025).

data_points.source_description: 3i Group FY2025 Annual Report; stock data aggregators
data_points.extraction_notes: EPS (TTM) of £6.49 (or 649p). Company profit of £5,294M for FY2025. NAV per share of 2,542p.

Infrastructure AUM of £6.3B (the third-party managed component). Down from £6.7B at FY2024, reflecting sale of European Operational Projects Fund capability. Note: 3i's PE portfolio (~£45B+) is principally on balance sheet, not third-party FEAUM.

data_points.source_description: Universe file FIRM-021; 3i Group FY2025 Report
data_points.extraction_notes: Total AUM ~£38.7B (~$51.8B at period-end rates). Includes PE portfolio on balance sheet (Action dominant) + infrastructure third-party AUM. Note: not directly comparable to pure-play alt managers.

data_points.missing_reason: 3i does not report FRE as a metric. Fee income from infrastructure fund management was £61M in FY2025 (down from £68M in FY2024). Standalone FRE margin not disclosed.

data_points.source_description: Calculated from fee income £61M / infrastructure AUM £6.3B
data_points.extraction_notes: Approximate: £61M fee income / £6.3B infrastructure AUM = ~97 bps. Only covers infrastructure third-party AUM, not the principal PE portfolio.

data_points.missing_reason: 3i does not report FRE. Market cap of ~$42.5B (~£33B) reflects the entire balance sheet (dominated by Action PE investment), not fee-earning business. P/FRE not meaningful for a principal investor model.
Eurazeo does not report DE/share in alt-manager framework. Hybrid model with principal investments + third-party AUM. Reports NAV and net income.

data_points.extraction_notes: Fee Paying AUM of €27.8B as of June 30, 2025, up 8% over 12 months. Third-party FPAUM up 10%. FY2025 full-year results expected March 11, 2026.
Group AUM of €36.8B as of June 30, 2025, up 4% over 12 months. Third-party AUM at €27.5B (+10%). From 9M 2025 update: AUM reached €37.4B, with 3P AUM up 11%.
FRE margin of 35.5% for FY2024, up 110bps from FY2023. FRE of €150M for full year 2024. H1 2025 FRE margin was 34.8% (€73.3M).
FRE up 11% YoY to €150M in FY2024 compared to FY2023.

data_points.missing_reason: Comp-to-revenue ratio not separately disclosed in available search results. Eurazeo employs 438 people (as of H1 2025).

data_points.extraction_notes: 438 employees / €27.8B FPAUM = ~15.8 employees per €1B FPAUM. Note this includes balance sheet investment team, not just 3P AUM management.

Market cap ~€3.07B (as of Feb 2026). LTM FRE of €150M (FY2024 — FY2025 not yet available). P/FRE = €3,070M / €150M = ~20.5x. Note: market cap includes balance sheet investment portfolio NAV, so P/FRE overstates the multiple on the asset management business.

data_points.missing_reason: Ridgepost Capital (P10) reports Adjusted Net Income (ANI) rather than DE. FY2025 ANI was $30.2M, fully diluted ANI/share $0.26 (vs $0.30 in FY2024). GAAP net income was $23.0M. ANI used as DE proxy where applicable.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Feb 12, 2026)
data_points.extraction_notes: Q4 2025 GAAP EPS of $0.09 (diluted). FY2025 GAAP net income of $23.0M. Annual GAAP EPS ~$0.20 (estimated from $23M / ~115M diluted shares).

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Feb 12, 2026)
data_points.extraction_notes: Fee-Paying AUM of $29.4B as of December 31, 2025. Up 15% YoY.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Feb 12, 2026)
data_points.extraction_notes: Total AUM over $43B as of December 31, 2025.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Feb 12, 2026)
data_points.extraction_notes: Fee-Paying AUM up 15% YoY to $29.4B.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Feb 12, 2026)
data_points.extraction_notes: Record organic gross new fee-paying asset fundraising of $5.1B in 2025. Beginning total AUM ~$37.4B (approx). Ratio: $5.1B/$37.4B ≈ 14%.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release; stock data
data_points.extraction_notes: Q4 2025 FRE margin of 48%. For FY2025: FRE of $39.0M on fee-related revenue of ~$297.3M implies a full-year margin of only ~13%. However, fee-related revenue includes pass-through items. Using Q4 annualized as better proxy.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital Q4 & FY2025 Earnings Release (Feb 12, 2026)
data_points.extraction_notes: FRE declined from $42.7M (FY2024) to $39.0M (FY2025), a -8.7% decline despite AUM growth. ANI also declined from $35.3M to $30.2M.

data_points.source_description: Ridgepost Capital company data; FY2025 results
data_points.extraction_notes: 267 employees / $29.4B FPAUM = ~9.1 employees per $1B FPAUM.
$29.4B / 267 employees = ~$110M FEAUM per employee.
Market cap ~$947M (as of Feb 2026). LTM FRE of $39.0M. P/FRE = $947M / $39M = ~24.3x.

data_points.source_description: Calculated: Market cap $947M / ANI $30.2M (as DE proxy)
data_points.extraction_notes: Using ANI of $30.2M as DE proxy. P/DE = $947M / $30.2M = ~31.4x. GAAP net income of $23M would give P/E of ~41x.

data_points.missing_reason: Antin does not report DE/share. Reports underlying EBITDA and underlying net income. FY2024 underlying EBITDA of €186.9M, underlying EBITDA margin 59%.

data_points.extraction_notes: Fee-Paying AUM of €21.8B as of June 30, 2025, up 6.2% YoY. FY2024 Fee-Paying AUM was €21.6B, up 7.3% YoY.
Fee-Paying AUM of €21.6B as of December 31, 2024, up 7.3% YoY.
Total AUM of €33.3B as of September 30, 2025, up 3.2% over trailing 12 months. FY2024 AUM was €33.3B, up 7.2% YoY.
Fee-Paying AUM up 7.3% YoY in FY2024.

Underlying EBITDA margin of 59% in FY2024, down 3pp from 2023. Underlying EBITDA of €186.9M. Note: Antin reports EBITDA rather than FRE; used as closest proxy. ~98% of revenue is long-term contracted management fees.
Underlying EBITDA grew 6.5% YoY to €186.9M in FY2024. Revenue grew 6.4%.
Personnel expenses of €92.5M in 2024 on total revenue of ~€317M (implied from EBITDA margin). Ratio: €92.5M/€317M ≈ 29%. Up 24.7% YoY driven by hiring and compensation increases.
Effective management fee rate of 1.34% (134 bps) in H1 2025. Broadly stable: 1.33% in FY2024, 1.32% in FY2023. Pure infrastructure mandate commands premium fee rates.

data_points.source_description: Antin FY2024 Results; company employee data
data_points.extraction_notes: ~240 employees / €21.6B FPAUM = ~11.1 employees per €1B FPAUM.
€21.6B / 240 employees = ~€90M FPAUM per employee.

Market cap ~€1.97B (Jan 2026). LTM underlying EBITDA of €186.9M (FY2024) used as FRE proxy. P/FRE ≈ €1,970M/€186.9M = ~10.5x. FY2025 EBITDA guided at ~€160M (FX headwinds); using that would give ~12.3x.

data_points.missing_reason: Enterprise value requires net debt figure. Market cap available (€1.97B) but balance sheet data not extracted from search results.
Antin guided FY2025 EBITDA down to ~€160M to reflect FX headwinds (EUR/USD impact on non-EUR AUM). Constant-currency revenue growth not separately disclosed but FX impact acknowledged as material.

FIRM-019_PAX.notes: 13 of 26 driver+dependent metrics populated. Missing metrics require 20-F filing for detailed breakdowns (comp, G&A, headcount, asset class AUM). P/FRE and P/DE computed. FX impact documented.

FIRM-020_MF.notes: 6 of 26 metrics populated. Wendel's holding company model makes many alt-manager metrics inapplicable. DE/share not reported. P/FRE misleading due to mixed business model. Rapid inorganic growth via IK Partners + Monroe complicates comparability.

FIRM-021_III.notes: 4 of 26 metrics populated. 3i's principal investor model is fundamentally different from pure-play alt managers. Most VDA metrics inapplicable. Fee income only from £6.3B infrastructure third-party AUM. PE is entirely balance-sheet driven.

FIRM-022_RF.notes: 6 of 26 metrics populated. FY2025 full-year results not yet released (expected March 11, 2026). H1 2025 used for AUM data; FY2024 for FRE. Hybrid model complicates P/FRE interpretation.

FIRM-023_PX.notes: 11 of 26 metrics populated. Pure-play alt with good data availability. FRE declined -8.7% despite 15% FPAUM growth, suggesting cost pressure. ANI used as DE proxy.

FIRM-024_ANTIN.notes: 10 of 26 metrics populated. Pure-play infrastructure with premium fee rates (134 bps). FY2025 full-year results not yet released. EBITDA used as FRE proxy. FX headwinds reduced 2025 guidance.
